title: Fehlaaur'aitel'loro
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Sith Empire |image=[[File:Fehlauur.jpg]] |name=Fehlaaur'aitel'loro
(Fehlaaur) |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]][[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Red |skin=Blue |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]][[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] *[[Fel Empire]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] **[[Imperial Diplomatic Corps/Legends|Imperial Diplomatic Corps]] **[[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]][[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]] **[[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]][[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We each govern a sector. We each have power.|Fehlaaur to the Moff Council|}} '''Fehlaaur'aitel'loro''', also known as '''Fehlaaur''', was a [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. He was the Ascendancy's liaison to the [[Fel Empire]] and was placed in the service of the [[Imperial Diplomatic Corps/Legends|Imperial Diplomatic Corps]] as a sign of solidarity between the two governments. In [[127 ABY]], when the Ascendancy received word of an impending [[Sith–Imperial War|conflict]] between the Empire and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], they attempted to recall Fehlaaur. To ensure the Ascendancy remained bound to the Empire, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] granted Fehlaaur the title of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], giving him a place on the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], and promoted him to [[Director/Legends|director]] of the Imperial Diplomatic Corps. At the end of the Sith–Imperial War, Fehlaaur was among the Moffs present when [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] Roan Fel, and assumed the throne. During the proceeding [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Fehlaaur continued to serve Krayt's Empire on the Moff Council. In [[137 ABY]], Krayt tasked the Moffs with devoting their resources to finding former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]], a descendant of the famous [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. After the Sith succeeded in capturing Skywalker, the Moffs were ordered to return to their previously assigned tasks. Not content with being dismissed so easily, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]] ordered Fehlaaur and the other Moffs to find out all they could about Skywalker, wanting to know where the former Jedi was, dead or alive. Following the [[Battle on Had Abbadon|apparent death of Darth Krayt]] at the hands of Skywalker and his allies, Fehlaaur and the High Moff Council met to discuss reports that Emperor-in-exile Fel had planned a secret [[Mission to Agamar|meeting]] with the Jedi on the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]. Veed intended for the Empire to capture Fel at the meeting and ordered the Moffs to prepare their forces for immediate departure. Moffs Yage and Fehlaaur were given command of the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]], leading the the strikeforce on the [[Operation: Thunderstroke|mission]] to capture Fel. Following Krayt's return, Fehlaaur was given the position of [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] and was present during the [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attack]] on the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] of the Jedi Order. ==Biography== ===Sith–Imperial War=== {{Quote|We could have chosen otherwise. Instead we forced that partnership on Roan Fel just as we forced the war with the Alliance. We ''wanted'' war.''"
"''But Roan Fel did not. And now ''we'' reap the consequences.|Nyna Calixte and Fehlaaur|}} [[File:LegacyImps.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Fehlaaur and the Moff Council at the attack on Emperor Fel.]] When the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] Fehlaaur'aitel'loro, commonly known simply as Fehlaaur, traveled from the Chiss [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]] to the [[Fel Empire|Imperial]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] as a diplomatic envoy from the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], he was placed in the service of the [[Imperial Diplomatic Corps/Legends|Imperial Diplomatic Corps]] as a sign of solidarity between the two governments. In [[127 ABY]], after the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]-backed [[Ossus Project]]—the project to have the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Terraforming/Legends|terraform]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] ravaged by their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]—was sabotaged, the Empire seized the opportunity created by the loss of confidence in the current galactic government, the Galactic Alliance, to attempt to restore the Empire as the dominant galactic power. When news of this reached the Chiss Ascendancy, they attempted to recall Fehlaaur to Csilla, wanting to avoid having one of their own drawn into the impending [[Sith–Imperial War|conflict]]. In order to ensure that the Ascendancy remained bound to the Empire, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] granted Fehlaaur the title of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], giving him a place on the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], and promoted him to [[Director/Legends|director]] of the Imperial Diplomatic Corps. At the end of the Sith–Imperial War, in the aftermath of the [[Massacre at Ossus]], where the Empire's allies, the [[One Sith]], decimated the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]], Fehlaaur was among the Moffs present when [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] Roan Fel, and assumed the throne. The Moff Council was unaware, however, that the [[Human/Legends|Human]] whom Krayt had killed to assume the throne was not Roan Fel; he was a [[Roan Fel's double|decoy]]. The real Fel had escaped, and was heading to Bastion to form a [[Empire-in-exile|government-in-exile]]. As the new [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]], Krayt demanded that [[High Moff]] [[Morlish Veed]] swear loyalty to him, followed by Fehlaaur and the rest of the council. ===Second Imperial Civil War=== {{Quote|What ''about'' this Skywalker? What have our searches turned up? Moff Fehlaaur—anything from the Diplomatic Corps? Or from the Chiss?''"
"''The Diplomatic Corps knew his father Kol but knows nothing of his son. The Ascendancy claims no knowledge of him.|Veed and Fehlaaur|}} [[File:FehlaaurYage.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Fehlaaur and Rulf Yage]] In the aftermath of the Sith–Imperial War, during the proceeding [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Fehlaaur continued to serve on the Moff Council. In [[137 ABY]], Krayt tasked the Moffs with devoting their resources to finding a descendant of the famous [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]], whom Krayt sought for his healing ability. After Krayt succeeded in capturing the former Jedi, his subordinate [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maladi]] contacted the Moff High Council in their meeting chambers on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and ordered the Moffs, as well as the agencies under their command, to return to their previously assigned tasks. Not content with being dismissed so easily, Grand Admiral Veed asked Fehlaaur what information the Diplomatic Corps and the Chiss had on Skywalker. Fehlaaur informed the council that neither the Diplomatic Corps nor the Ascendancy had any knowledge of Skywalker, causing fellow Moff [[Rulf Yage]] to accuse the Ascendancy of withholding information. Fehlaaur was not drawn into a confrontation, however, instead dismissing the claim. Unhappy about their lack of power under the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], the Moffs attempted to blame Moff [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]]—the [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]]—for their current predicament. Calixte responded by reminding them that they had all chosen to ally with the Sith; they had all wanted the war. However, Fehlaaur pointed out that Roan Fel had not, and now the Moffs were suffering the consequences. Veed, having had enough of the arguing, ordered the Moffs to find out all they could about Skywalker, wanting to know where the Sith were keeping the former Jedi, dead or alive. After Cade Skywalker and his allies succeeded in their [[Battle on Had Abbadon|mission to kill Darth Krayt]] and Veed—who believed Krayt to be still alive, but in stasis—was made [[Regent/Legends|Regent]] of the Empire by Darth Wyyrlok, Fehlaaur and the High Moff Council met in their chambers on Coruscant to discuss reports that Emperor-in-exile Fel was planning to secretly [[Mission to Agamar|meet]] with the Jedi on the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]. Veed planned for the Empire to capture Fel at the meeting and put the exiled Emperor on trial to end the Bastion resistance. Fehlaaur, assuming the intelligence was acquired by Nyna Calixte, asked her where the information had come from. However, Veed himself had gained the intelligence and refused to give his source. The Regent ordered Fehlaaur and the other Moffs to prepare their forces for immediate departure before dismissing them.[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] Moffs Yage and Fehlaaur were given command of the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]], with Fehlaaur serving under Yage as the [[Task force/Legends|task force's]] [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]]. Commanding from aboard the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[War Hammer]]'', Fehlaaur and Yage led the the strikeforce on the [[Operation: Thunderstroke|mission]] to capture Roan Fel. Yage was unhappy with the number of Sith that composed the First Sith Imperial Strikeforce, commenting to Fehlaaur that he had become a leader of monsters. The mission was ultimately unsuccessful, as Emperor Fel was able to escape; however, the Sith forces were able to capture Fel's daughter, [[Marasiah Fel]].[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] ===Director of Imperial Intelligence=== {{Quote|Moff Fehlaaur, ''you'' will now assume the role of Chief Intelligence Officer.''"
"''Y-yes, Lord Krayt. As you command.|Krayt to Fehlaaur|}} Following the return of Lord Krayt[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]] and after Moff Calixte was revealed to be a traitor to the Sith Empire,[[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy — War'' 2]] Fehlaaur was given Calixte's position as Director of Imperial Intelligence. Having learned the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] of the Jedi Order was located on the planet [[Taivas]], Darth Krayt planned an [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attack]] on the temple, and Fehlaaur joined the fleet for the battle. As the Sith–Imperial fleet began its attack on the Jedi compound, they discovered that a build up of charge by the planet's rock formations deflected energy, causing the Sith fleet to lose fighters to friendly fire. Fehlaaur was blamed for the lack of prior knowledge of these rock formations, as his operatives had failed to warn the Sith commanders of them, though the Chiss blamed the situation on the state Moff Calixte had left Imperial Intelligence. With the aid of Lord Krayt's [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith troopers]], Krayt's forces were able to win the battle against the combined forces of the Jedi, Alliance Remnant, and the Empire-in-exile, though in the process Moff Geist was killed by friendly fire while Moff Yage defected to Roan Fel's side, following his [[Gunn Yage|daughter's]] footsteps.[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] ==Personality and traits== [[File:VeedFehlaaur.jpg|thumb|left|Moffs Veed and Fehlaaur]] {{Quote|Repeating an allegation doesn't ''prove'' it, Moff Yage—coincidences can always be linked in a suggestive manner.|Fehlaaur|}} Fehlaaur was a Chiss with [[Color/Legends|blue]] skin, black [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Compared to his fellow members of the Moff Council, Fehlaaur was quite aloof and mild-mannered. Unlike the other Moffs on the council, Fehlaaur remained cautious and would not surrender to his emotions. When Moff Yage accused the Ascendancy of withholding information, Fehlaaur did not enter into a confrontation, instead pointing out that there was no evidence to back the claim. Along with Moff [[Konrad Rus]] of the [[Imperial Mission]], Fehlaaur helped keep the balance within the council, often serving as the counterpoint to the more aggressive Moffs. Serving as a diplomat for the Chiss Ascendancy, Fehlaaur was a polyglot capable of speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Bothese]], [[Cheunh/Legends|Cheunh]], and [[High Galactic (language)|High Galactic]]. ==Behind the scenes== Fehlaaur first appeared in the first issue of ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'', [[Legacy (2006) 1|''Broken, Part 1'']], released in [[June]] [[2006]], where he was an unnamed Moff present at the attack on Emperor Fel. He was identified by the name Fehlaaur in the fifteenth issue of the series, [[Legacy (2006) 15|''Claws of the Dragon, Part 2'']] released in [[August]] [[2007]]. Both issues were written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]], and penciled by Duursema. He was later featured in the supplement to the series numbered [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|zero-and-a-half]],[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] and given an entry in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{CSWECite|I|259|Fehlaaur}} Fehlaaur was given his full Chiss name, Fehlaaur'aitel'loro, and statistics for gameplay, in the ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' released in [[March]] [[2009]]. Fehlaaur returned in the [[Legacy — War 3|third issue]] of the sequel to ''Legacy'' series, [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']]. The name and appearance of Fehlaaur was based upon a fan who donated $2,700 at the 2006 [[Wikipedia:Child's Play (charity)|Child's Play]] charity dinner to make his appearance in a ''[[Star Wars]]'' comic.{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=boards/viewtopic.php?t=12624&sid=e4c0febbea5e27041630fd3a2e3d0e0a|thread=STAR WARS appearance auctioned for charity}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Fehlaaur'aitel'loro}} *[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] {{1stID|Fehlaaur}} *[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] *[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]] *[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|89|The Art of War (Star Wars Insider 89)}} {{Po}} *[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{1stID|Fehlaaur'aitel'loro}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Moffs130ABY}} {{Interlang |de=Fehlaaur'aitel'loro |pl=Fehlaaur'aitel'loro |fi=Fehlaaur'aitel'loro }} [[Category:Chiss]] [[Category:Diplomats]] [[Category:Imperial Intelligence Directors]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Moff Council members]] [[Category:Moffs of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Moffs of the Fel Empire]]
